description:
Integrate destination-passing-style (DPS) codegen and a hybrid (Cheney + RC) GC into fz. DPS lowers result construction so callees write into a caller-supplied slot; the RC arena unlocks future FBIP reuse rewrites without requiring a wholesale GC rewrite.

## The architectural insight

DPS is not a new system — it is a new variant of the per-spec ABI that already exists. `ArgRepr` (Tagged/RawI64/RawF64) gains `Dst(HeapKind)`. `Cont` gains `dst: Option<DstSig>`. The decision lives in `build_fn_signature` (ir_codegen.rs:896), the same place spec ABIs are already chosen.

DPS and RC are independent: a destination can live in Cheney. RC earns its keep only when paired with uniqueness/FBIP. The plan splits into three independent arcs.

## Arcs

- C+D+F+E (DPS-only): ships measurable CLIF reductions allocating from Cheney. No RC needed.
- B (hybrid GC arena): introduces alloc_owned + refcount machinery. Enables FBIP.
- G (FBIP, gated by spike G0): uniqueness inference + in-place reuse rewrites. Requires both DPS and RC.

## Goals

1. CLIF is monotonic-or-better at every ticket. No regressions.
2. Three-path parity preserved by construction (interp ignores dst).
3. Removals are first-class tickets (E1-E3).

## Out of scope (deferrals)

- Per-callsite ABI specialization — defers to fz-ul4.29.2.1.
- RC for HeapKind::Closure — cycle risk, unpredictable sharing, low payoff.
- RC for HeapKind::Map — insertion-order linear-lookup maps do not benefit from FBIP.
- A general FBIP rewrite engine — kind-by-kind rewrites with per-kind benchmarks instead.

## Dependencies on existing tickets

- fz-o0u (tag scheme cleanup) should land before C-phase.
- fz-6rw (16-byte cons cells) blocks F4 — cell layout decides RC-for-cons feasibility.
- fz-h9f (generational GC) is orthogonal; composes with hybrid Cheney/RC.
- fz-duq (branching-construct lowering) blocks D1.

## Walkthrough findings already baked in

- D1 fixture must be a heap-result case ({1,2} / {3,4}), not int arms.
- Sharp-spec DPS deferred; only any-key specs targeted in v1.
- E1 (HeapKind::Float) is an audit, not a confident deletion.
- F4 explicitly depends on fz-6rw cell layout decision.
- Externs invariant: ArgRepr::Dst must never appear on extern signatures.

See child tickets for the atomic DAG.
title: fz-dps: destination-passing codegen + hybrid GC foundation
